steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: Before the blank is the independent clause "...two other Black Americans who served in Congress." After the blank comes a list naming those two people: "Ralph Harold Metcalfe of Illinois and Alton R. Waldon Jr. of New York."

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content:
A colon is the standard mark for introducing a list or specification that follows an independent clause — exactly the function here.

A semicolon (B) joins independent clauses, a period (C) breaks the sentence apart leaving a fragment, and no punctuation (D) creates an awkward run-on.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
